event: Awakening on the background of the Crucifixion
symbols: winged figure before the cross — the soul awakened through Christ's sacrifice, new creation born of the cross
crucified figure behind — Christ's death as the precondition for spiritual awakening
outstretched arms mirroring the cross — the one being saved conformed to the image of the Savior
white luminous wings — the transformation from earthly to spiritual, mortality put on immortality
halo on the awakening figure — sanctification achieved through the blood of the cross
description: A tall vertical composition showing a standing figure with large white wings, arms raised and bent behind the head, positioned directly before a dark crucifix bearing a smaller figure. The winged figure has a luminous body rendered in warm flesh tones with green and pink highlights, with a faint halo. The large white wings spread out to fill the width of the composition. The background is a textured mosaic of pale and warm tones suggesting radiant light. The overall effect is of spiritual emergence or awakening.
theological_description: This painting juxtaposes the Crucifixion with a figure of spiritual awakening, visualizing Paul's theology that 'if anyone is in Christ, the new creation has come: the old has gone, the new is here' (2 Corinthians 5:17). The winged figure standing before the cross embodies the soul transformed by Christ's sacrifice — what was dead in sin has been made alive (Ephesians 2:4-5). The arms raised behind the head mirror the crucified posture, illustrating that believers are 'crucified with Christ' so that they 'no longer live, but Christ lives in them' (Galatians 2:20). The luminous wings suggest the resurrected body that Paul describes: 'It is sown a natural body; it is raised a spiritual body' (1 Corinthians 15:44). The cross is both the instrument of death and the doorway to new life — 'through the cross' the world is crucified to the believer and the believer to the world (Galatians 6:14).

scripture_refs.reason: New creation in Christ — the awakening figure represents spiritual rebirth through the cross
Crucified with Christ, yet alive — the figure before the cross embodies this paradox
Sown a natural body, raised a spiritual body — the winged luminous form
Made alive with Christ even when dead in transgressions — the awakening motif

_meta.title: Awakening on the background of the Crucifixion
